---
id: list-phone-control-targets
title: "List phone-control targets"
description: "Returns the registered phones that FS PBX can control for one extension."
sidebar_label: "List phone-control targets"
hide_title: true
hide_table_of_contents: true
api: 
sidebar_class_name: "get api-method"
info_path: docs/api/v1/fs-pbx-api
custom_edit_url: null
hide_send_button: true
---

import MethodEndpoint from "@theme/ApiExplorer/MethodEndpoint";
import ParamsDetails from "@theme/ParamsDetails";
import RequestSchema from "@theme/RequestSchema";
import StatusCodes from "@theme/StatusCodes";
import OperationTabs from "@theme/OperationTabs";
import TabItem from "@theme/TabItem";
import Heading from "@theme/Heading";

<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"List phone-control targets"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"get"}
  path={"/api/v1/domains/{domain_uuid}/phone-control/targets"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



Returns the registered phones that FS PBX can control for one extension.
Use the returned `agent` value with either the click-to-dial or phone-control
action endpoint. Each target also lists its supported phone-control actions.
Use `vendor`, `lan_ip`, or a `registration_call_ids` value when needed.
See [Supported phones and actions](https://www.fspbx.com/docs/additional-information/phone-control/#supported-phones-and-actions)
for vendor-specific capabilities and behavior.

Access rules:
- Caller must have access to the target domain.
- Caller must have the `phone_control_view` permission.

<Heading
  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
  className={"openapi-tabs__heading"}
  children={"Request"}
>
</Heading>

  parameters={[{"in":"query","name":"extension","description":"Extension number to inspect.","example":"1001","required":true,"schema":{"type":"string","description":"Extension number to inspect.","example":"1001"}},{"in":"path","name":"domain_uuid","description":"The domain UUID.","example":"4018f7a3-8e0a-47bb-9f4f-04b1313e0e1b","required":true,"schema":{"type":"string"}}]}
>
  
</ParamsDetails>
